items.title: Bangladesh to play one-off Lord's Test against England in 2027
items.summary: Bangladesh will return to the Home of Cricket for the first time in 17 years when they play a one-off Test against England at Lord's from May 28 to June 1 next year, with the England and Wales Cricket Board (ECB) confirming the full itinerary for Bangladesh men's and women's tours in its 2027 international schedule announced on Thursday.

items.title: Konstas, Kellaway, Patterson named in CA XI to face Bangladesh
items.summary: Rocchiccioli, Philippe, Doran also included for the three-day tour game

items.title: Mahedi set to face sanction after disciplinary hearing
items.summary: Bangladesh Cricket Board's (BCB) disciplinary committee summoned all-rounder Mahedi Hasan for a hearing over his criticism of the team management during last month's T20I series against Australia. The all-rounder submitted his statement to the committee and is set to be sanctioned for breaching his contract.

items.title: Can Tigers’ batting pass the Australian test?
items.summary: Since March 2024, both Bangladesh and Australia’s pacers have averaged over 140kph, establishing them as the two fastest pace attacks in the current game.

items.title: Lack of practice matches behind batting debacle in Zim, says Ashraful
items.summary: Bangladesh batting coach Mohammad Ashraful believes the absence of practice matches before the Zimbabwe tour left the batters struggling to adapt to the conditions, contributing to the team's disappointing performances.

items.title: Aminul hopeful of improved sports diplomacy through India series
items.summary: India last toured Bangladesh in 2022 for a three-match ODI series and a two-match Test series.

items.title: Cummins, Hazlewood, Lyon return to face Bangladesh
items.summary: Australia's veterans named in a 13-man squad with Cameron Green and Beau Webster both included

items.title: Hridoy pleased with T20I series win despite 'injuries and limited options'
items.summary: Bangladesh were without Mustafizur Rahman and Shoriful Islam for the entire series, and lost Nahid Rana for the decider, which they won by four wickets

items.title: Bangladesh win dramatic finale to clinch T20I series against Zimbabwe
items.summary: Tanzid Hasan anchored the chase with a half-century as Bangladesh won with two balls to spare

items.title: Zimbabwe opt to bat in series decider
items.summary: Bangladesh were without Nahid Rana, who suffered a side strain on Friday

items.title: Bangladesh eye series win to finish forgettable tour
items.summary: After winning the one-off Test and the ODI series, Zimbabwe have the chance to clinch the T20I series too
